free_text: properties from measurements at the DANCE facility
The Lujan Center at the Los Alamos
Neutrons are produced by spallation when 800
MeV protons strike a tungsten target at 20 Hz
repetition rate. Flight Path 14 views a water-filled
backscatter moderator. Neutrons from 1 eV up to 1 MeV
were utilized in the present experiment.
DANCE is a multi-detector array composed of
Two primary samples during the measurements: both 1x1
thicknesses. These values correspond to areal
atoms/b, respectively. The thin sample was used for
low En region to reduce self-attenuation effects and
the thicker sample was used at larger En for improved
Center at the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center.
There exist several self-attenuation effects, which
increase with the thickness of the sample. The
authors used SAMMY to derive corrections for these
The capture cross section as a function of neutron
energy En were calculated in the thin-sample
Final uncertainties of the cross section
derived cross section are related to the number of
counts measured with DANCE and the employed beam
Data were provided by I.Knapova on October 17, 2025.
To facilitate the comparison between experimental and
simulated spectra, authors have constructed the mean
sum-energy and MSC spectra from the normalized
